pattern: Setting a chosen element to 1 destroys every stable run through it, so binary search the target length and greedily count the breakers needed.
hints: Changing an element to 1 forces the gcd of any subarray crossing it to 1, breaking stability there.
For a fixed cap L, sweep left to right tracking the distinct gcd-runs ending at each index and drop a breaker whenever a stable run would exceed L.
Binary search the smallest L whose required breaker count is at most maxC.

edge_inputs.description: two changes break a run of three
two separate runs cannot both shrink
no budget keeps the full run

stress.description: small arrays so the subset oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 7)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    maxC = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [nums, maxC]
